UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: base cost from tech level
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Provides a fallback base cost derived from the weapon's tech level. Ensures all weapons have a customization cost even if they lack a craftable recipe. Neolithic uses wood, medieval uses steel, industrial uses steel and components, and spacer and above use plasteel and advanced components

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: base cost from recipe
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Replaces the tech-level fallback with the weapon's actual crafting recipe costs, including both fixed ingredients and stuff costs. Only acts when the weapon has a craftable base definition.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: quality multiplier
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Scales costs based on weapon quality. Higher-quality weapons cost more to customize. Includes the base 50% cost fraction so each trait costs half the weapon's full recipe value before quality scaling.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: negative trait material downgrade
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Downgrades cost materials by one technology level for negative traits. Spacer components become industrial, plasteel becomes steel, steel becomes wood. Reflects that negative modifications use cruder, cheaper techniques.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: toxic component swap
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Replaces components with herbal medicine (3x the component count), reflecting the organic toxins needed. If no components are present, converts 70% of base materials to herbal medicine by market value.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: incendiary component swap
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Replaces components with chemfuel (15x the component count), reflecting the volatile fuel needed. If no components are present, converts 70% of base materials to chemfuel by market value.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: EMP value split
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Removes spacer components, then converts 70% of base material costs into industrial components by market value, representing the electronic parts needed for EMP modifications.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: lightweight swap
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Replaces 40% of wood costs with birdskin, representing the feather tails used on arrows.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: flarestriker value split
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Converts 70% of base material costs into bioferrite by market value, reflecting the exotic fuel source needed for flare launchers.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: heavy scrap flat cost
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Replaces all costs with a single steel slag chunk. Scrap modifications are crude and cheap — just bolt on some junk.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: charge weapons (category-gated)
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Converts all costs into advanced components by market value for charge-type weapon traits, representing the spacer technology required for pulse and beam modifications.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: charge weapons (unconditional)
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Converts all costs into advanced components by market value for traits involving advanced spacer technology, regardless of weapon category.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: akimbo cost multiplier
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Doubles all costs to reflect that akimbo adds a second rendered weapon and doubles the fire rate. This counteracts the base 50% cost fraction to bring the total to full recipe cost.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: grip component removal
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Removes industrial and spacer components from the cost. Grip modifications are simple mechanical adjustments that don't require complex parts.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: comfort material conversion
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Converts half of all cost materials to thrumbofur by count, reflecting the luxurious padding and wrapping used for comfort modifications.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: ornamental conversion
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Removes components, then converts half of remaining costs to silver by count. Ornamental work is decorative craftsmanship, not technical engineering.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: inlay component removal
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Removes industrial and spacer components from the cost. Inlays are purely cosmetic modifications that require only raw materials, not complex parts.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: material override fallback
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Detects material names in the trait label (e.g. "gold" in "Gold Inlay") and replaces raw resource costs with that material. Acts as a catch-all for traits without a specific thematic rule.

UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.label: cost material pruning
UniqueWeaponsUnbound.TraitCostRuleDef.description: Limits the cost to at most 3 material types to prevent UI overflow. When there are more than 3, the cheapest materials by unit price are trimmed first.
